text.title: Risk Threshold Policy Failure Modes
text.summary: 6 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Band routes to 'the steering committee' without a named chair.
antipattern.root-cause: Group inboxes diffuse accountability; nobody owns the response.
antipattern.fix: Replace with named role + person; add deputy fallback.

antipattern.symptom: Band defined but no time-to-escalation SLA.
antipattern.root-cause: Without SLA, escalation slips silently; sponsor cannot hold PM accountable.
antipattern.fix: Reject policy without SLA per band; require explicit hours/days.

antipattern.symptom: PM escalates everything to red, even amber-band risks.
antipattern.root-cause: Steering-committee fatigue; future red escalations lose credibility.
antipattern.fix: Apply route-risk.py mechanically; the policy decides, not the PM in the moment.

antipattern.symptom: Policy signed at kickoff and never refreshed; team changed.
antipattern.root-cause: Named targets no longer in role; escalation lands in dead inbox.
antipattern.fix: Mandatory refresh at milestone OR 12 weeks; pre-commit hook flags policy age.

antipattern.symptom: Risks reach red and stay red; no defined path down.
antipattern.root-cause: Register becomes a museum of past worries.
antipattern.fix: Each band requires explicit de-escalation criteria signed off by named target.

antipattern.symptom: No policy exists; PM hesitates and routes mid-incident.
antipattern.root-cause: Delay damages sponsor relationship ('why didn't you tell me sooner?').
antipattern.fix: Policy MUST exist at kickoff; co-signed.
